steps.title: Identify the claim's weakness
steps.content: The team is using a ten-year-old survey about willingness to walk, plus general findings that sidewalks/crosswalks influence willingness. The risk: conditions may have changed, making the old survey unreliable.

steps.title: Pick the supporting finding
steps.content:
Choice A: sidewalks and crosswalks in the neighborhood have increased substantially over those ten years. Combined with the general finding that paved infrastructure increases willingness to walk, this supports placing the stop based on residents now being willing to walk farther than the old survey indicated.

B is general weather behavior; C is about cyclists, not pedestrians; D is rider satisfaction, irrelevant to placement.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
